No human lives were lost when the Tacoma Narrows Bridge collapsed. One dog perished. The collapse was caused by wind-induced aeroelastic flutter, not an earthquake.
Humans use 100% of their brain. The idea that we only use 10% is a persistent neurological myth without scientific support.
The Colosseum was completed in 80 CE, centuries before Christianity became influential in the Roman Empire. Therefore, its architecture was not influenced by the adoption of Christianity in 313 CE.

Charles Darwin did not visit Easter Island. His finch observations, crucial for his theory of natural selection, were made on the Galapagos Islands, noting beak variations adapted to different food sources.

The printing press enabled rapid, widespread dissemination of Reformation ideas, pamphlets, and Bibles, undermining Church authority and fostering literacy, vital for the movement's success.
